findings.evidence: Lines 93-106 already define `Hom.ofGroupLike` under only coalgebra assumptions, while lines 178-203 prove the equivalence and simp lemmas only after line 112 adds `[Semiring C] [Bialgebra R C]` and line 120 specializes to `(1 : GroupLike R C)`.
findings.fix: Prove `Hom.groupLikeEquiv (g : GroupLike R C) : Hom R C M N ≃ (M →ₗ[R] N)` in `GroupLikeDef` using `Hom.ofGroupLike`, add the corresponding simp lemmas there, and define `trivialEquiv` as the specialization at `(1 : GroupLike R C)`.
findings.issue: `Hom.trivialEquiv` and its simp API are only stated for trivial comodules under `[Semiring C] [Bialgebra R C]`, even though the same equivalence holds for any `g : GroupLike R C` under the weaker `[AddCommMonoid C] [Module R C] [Coalgebra R C]`.

summary: The core comodule construction is now public at the natural coalgebra/group-like level, but the morphism equivalence API is still only provided for the bialgebraic `1` specialization. That leaves a readily available general theorem duplicated as trivial-only API.
